reasoning: While programmatic shingling recall is high (0.933), the LLM fidelity signals indicate major structural and visual failures that cannot be auto-approved. The fragmentation of the primary data table into ten separate, corrupted table elements and the total loss of critical visual content (maps and analytics dashboards) representing the core of the presentation slides necessitate manual correction.

concerns.analysis: On page 8 of the source, there is a list titled 'Zone Cities and Zips' containing two columns of 20 rows each (totaling 40 data points). The output attempts to format this data as a series of ten small tables ('Zone Cities and Zips Table 1' through 'Table 10'). Collectively, these tables contain only about 10 rows of data, meaning significantly more than 20% of the structured rows are missing from the table elements. concerns.source_pdf_quote: The 'Zone Cities and Zips' list on page 8 contains 20 rows of city/zip pairs in a two-column format (40 entries total).
concerns.output_quote: The ten 'Zone Cities and Zips' tables in Section 6 contain approximately 10 data rows in total, omitting the vast majority of the structured entries found in the source.

concerns.analysis: The data from the 'Zone Cities and Zips' section on page 8 is duplicated in the output. The content appears first as a long text string in a paragraph element at the beginning of Section 6, and is then immediately repeated across ten fragmented table elements (e.g., 'Zone Cities and Zips Table 1', 'Table 2', etc.) within the same section.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: The list of zip codes and cities on Page 8 (e.g., '27231 Cedar Grove', '27278 Hillsborough', '27882 Spring Hope').
concerns.output_quote: The text appears in the paragraph starting 'Zone Cities and Zips 27231 Cedar Grove...' and again in the cells of the tables that follow.

concerns.analysis: The multi-column grid of 'Zone Cities and Zips' on page 8 is severely corrupted. Instead of a single cohesive table or list, the pipeline fragmented it into ten separate 1-row tables. Furthermore, the column alignment is broken; for instance, 'Zone Cities and Zips Table 1' uses three headers ('Zip', 'City', 'City') for a two-column source layout, incorrectly merging a zip code and city name into a single cell (e.g., '27882 Spring Hope'). concerns.source_pdf_quote: The 4-column grid of Zip codes and Cities on page 8.
concerns.output_quote: The series of fragmented tables in Section 6, specifically 'Zone Cities and Zips Table 1' with headers [Zip, City, City] and content [27278, Hillsborough, 27882 Spring Hope].

concerns.analysis: The HTML output fails to render numerous meaningful images that constitute the core content of the presentation slides. Specifically, the map of the Durham Zone (page 8), the TV network logos for 'Campaign Airings' (page 9), and the screenshots of the Google Analytics dashboards (pages 10, 11, 12, and 13) are all missing. In the rendered HTML, these are replaced by large green rectangular placeholders, leaving the document without its primary data visualizations.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: The source PDF contains a map on page 8, network logos on page 9, and Google Analytics dashboard screenshots on pages 10, 11, 12, and 13.
concerns.output_quote: In the HTML, these sections (e.g., under the heading 'Webpage Analytics: Site Summary') contain only large green boxes instead of the images.
concerns.location: Presentation slides section, corresponding to PDF pages 8, 9, 10, 11, 12, and 13.

concerns.description: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
concerns.analysis: A minor text discrepancy was found in the fragmented 'Zone Cities and Zips' tables in Section 6. In 'Table 9', the zip code '27589' is associated with the city 'Stem'. However, the source document correctly identifies 'Stem' with zip code '27581'. This change alters the meaning of the data.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: The list on page 8 correctly pairs '27581' with 'Stem'.
concerns.output_quote: Zone Cities and Zips Table 9 incorrectly pairs zip code '27589' with 'Stem'.
concerns.location: Section 6, Page 8, Table element 'Zone Cities and Zips Table 9'

concerns.analysis: The output fails to extract the list of government URLs from the 'Top 10 Clicks' table on page 13. While the table is visible in the source, it is only provided as an image in the JSON output (Section 11), meaning all functional links are missing from the document's structural text.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: The list of URLs on Page 13 (e.g., https://durhamnc.gov/3774/City-Jobs-Careers).
concerns.output_quote: The absence of text or link elements in Section 11, which only contains an image.

action_items: Consolidate the ten fragmented 'Zone Cities and Zips' tables into a single accessible table with correct headers.
Verify and restore missing images, specifically the maps and analytics dashboards on pages 8-13.
Remove the duplicated text block preceding the 'Zone Cities and Zips' table.
Correct the zip code error for 'Stem' and ensure the 'Top 10 Clicks' table includes functional text-based links.
